mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening housing market that boosts aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 112 signals a weakening supply chain that compresses profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 112 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250K signals a weakening labor market that boosts risk-free rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 138K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a strengthening aggregate demand that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 3% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that boosts discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% and a 3-month increase in housing units starts above 1.2 million signals a strengthening aggregate demand and housing market that boosts equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with both GDPC1 > 3% and HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 112 and a 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250K signals a weakening supply chain and labor market that compresses profit margins and boosts risk-free rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with both INDPRO < 112 and PAYEMS < 138K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
